chunks.text: THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Present: Mr. Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry Mr. Justice Muhammad Jaffer Raza C. P. No. D-5693 of 2025 (Jamshed Alam & Others Vs. Federation of Pakistan & Others) Petitioners: Through Mr. Ramiz Nasim, Advocate. Respondents: Ms. Mehreen Ibrahim, Deputy Attorney General. Ms. Faryal Abida, Sub-Inspector FIA Immigration JIAP, Karachi. DSP Ayaz Ahmed Pandhiani FRO/SB, Karachi. SIP Jawaid Akhtar, FRO/SB Karachi. Date of hearing: 02.12.2025. Date of decision: 02.12.2025. O R D E R Muhammad Jaffer Raza J. – Learned Deputy Attorney General files para-wise comments on behalf of respondents No. 2 & 4 as well as respondents No. 3 & 5, the same are taken on record, copies whereof have been provided to the learned counsel appearing for the petitioners. It is evident from the comments furnished that the noted respondents have no objection to the grant of the instant petition subject to certain formalities enumerated therein. By consent, instant petition is disposed of in the following terms:- 1) The petitioners shall obtain travel permits from the High Commission of Bangladesh, Karachi. The same shall be verified from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for confirmation of their genuineness. 2) Petitioners are allowed to travel subject to further verification of their status, criminal record, CRO / CRI. 2 3) The Director General, NADRA Sindh is directed to verify the identity of the petitioners through biometric for NADRA registration. If the petitioners are registered in NADRA their CNICs may be cancelled / blocked. 4) The Director General, NADRA Sindh is further directed to provide certificate to the petitioners subject to the above. 5) After completion of all codal formalities as required under the Foreign Act, 1946, the Petitioners may be permitted to travel to their native country Bangladesh.

J U D G E J U D G E Arshad/
